title: Moving a column in your list within your dashboard
excerpt: I have created a dashboard with the table widget.  I am trying to move the columns with the hand icon, and it is not moving at all.  Need some help.

posts.author: jmosinski (Joe Mosinski)
posts.cookedHtml: I have created a dashboard with the table widget. I am trying to move the columns with the hand icon, and it is not moving at all. Need some help.

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Joe! Currently, the Table Widget does not support the ability to change the order of existing columns. If you need to change the order of the columns, you will want to navigate to the specific board to make those changes!

posts.author: jmosinski (Joe Mosinski)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Malia,

Thank you for your response. Is there a way to make software enhancement requests?

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, there is! This feedback can be submitted directly to a dedicated section of our community page. Learn more here

posts.author: meghan.tighe (Meghan Tighe)
posts.cookedHtml:
feature request is here:

Reorder columns in table view in dashboard Feature requests
It seems that changing the order of columns in the table widget is not supported at this time. This means that the order shown in the main table is the order in which the table widget on the dashboard is the order shown automatically. It would be amazing to change this order, just like in an item card for example.

posts.author: Lilamx51 (Lila)
posts.cookedHtml: when would this be available> this seems like very basic feature ..

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Lila! We certainly hear you on this! If you haven’t upvoted the feature request that Meghan shared above, add your vote now. As this is a feature request, there isn’t an identified ETA yet. However, the Product team continuously reviews the Feature Requests forum to track users’ feature requests to prioritize on the Product Roadmap.

I would recommend providing your feedback in the feature request to continue building a case for this!
